chief_complaint: A 90-year-old man presented with myocardial infarction and gastrointestinal bleeding.
history: The patient had severe acute-on-chronic anemia and active gastrointestinal bleeding. His chest pain resolved with blood transfusions, suggesting a type 2 myocardial infarction. He had severe coronary artery disease and bleeding angiodysplasias.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings were not provided.
labs: ECG: myocardial infarction. Bloods: severe acute-on-chronic anemia. Imaging: not provided.
